question_text: Two devices are used to heat a swimming pool. Device A has a power of 2000 W and runs for 5 hours. Device B has a power of 1500 W and runs for 8 hours. Which device transfers more energy, and by how much? Show your working.
correct_answer: Device B transfers more energy by 4,000,000 J (or 4 MJ)
explanation: Calculate energy for Device A: E = 2000 W × (5 × 3600) s = 36,000,000 J. Calculate energy for Device B: E = 1500 W × (8 × 3600) s = 43,200,000 J. Device B transfers more energy; the difference is 43,200,000 J - 36,000,000 J = 7,200,000 J (or 7.2 MJ). Although Device A has higher power, Device B runs for longer and transfers more total energy.
